phrases.intent_en: Ask where the main luxury shopping street is
phrases.text_local: Où se trouve la rue commerçante principale pour les boutiques de luxe ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo suh TROOV lah ROO koh-mair-SONT pran-see-PAL poor lay boo-TEEK duh LOKS
phrases.cultural_note: The Rue du Rhône is Geneva's premier luxury shopping street, lined with high-end watchmakers, jewellers and fashion houses between Place Bel-Air and the lake.

phrases.text_local: Y a-t-il des boutiques de montres suisses dans cette rue ?
phrases.pronunciation: ee-ah-TEEL day boo-TEEK duh MON-truh SWEES don SET ROO
phrases.cultural_note: The Rue du Rhône and adjacent streets host flagship stores for Rolex, Patek Philippe, Omega and other Swiss watch brands — Geneva is the world capital of watchmaking.

responses.english: There are several along the street.
responses.phonetic: eel ee ahn ah ploo-ZYUHR luh LOHN duh lah ROO
responses.french: Il y en a plusieurs le long de la rue.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about tax refund for tourists
phrases.text_local: Est-il possible d'obtenir un remboursement de TVA en tant que touriste ?
phrases.pronunciation: eh-TEEL poh-SEE-bluh dob-tuh-NEER un rom-boors-MON duh TAY-VAY on TOHN kuh too-REEST
phrases.cultural_note: Switzerland is not in the EU, so tourists from outside Switzerland can claim a VAT refund (7.7%) on purchases above CHF 300 at the airport on departure.

responses.english: At the checkout, with your passport.
responses.phonetic: ah lah KESS, ah-VEK voh-truh pass-POR
responses.french: À la caisse, avec votre passeport.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if a store does watch repairs
phrases.cultural_note: Many watchmakers on the Rue du Rhône offer in-house repair services, though turnaround times for complex pieces can take several weeks.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if they accept credit cards
phrases.cultural_note: Most luxury boutiques accept all major credit cards. Switzerland uses the Swiss franc (CHF), not the euro, though many tourist shops do accept euros at a poor exchange rate.
